title: Biochemists and biomedical scientists
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 40 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze chromosomes found in biological specimens to aid diagnoses and treatments for genetic diseases, including counting chromosome numbers and identifying structural abnormalities by viewing slides with microscopes.

Prepare biological specimens for chromosome examinations, including amniotic fluids, blood, and tumors; prepare slides of cell cultures following standard procedures; and select or prepare media for cell cultures using aseptic techniques.
Examine specimens to detect abnormalities, recognizing and reporting issues related to the color, size, shape, composition, or pattern of cells.

Harvest cell cultures using various substances, determine optimal time sequences and methods for manual or robotic cell harvests, and prepare cell samples using special staining techniques to differentiate cell components.

Communicate test results or technical information to patients, physicians, family members, or researchers; summarize test results and report to appropriate authorities; and document specimens by verifying patients' and specimens' information.

Perform tests to evaluate blood or tissue samples, analyzing laboratory findings to ensure conformity to specifications and conducting chemical analyses of body fluids to detect abnormalities or diseases.

Select appropriate methods of specimen collection, preservation, or transport; compile materials for pathologists including surgical drafts and requisitions; and maintain effective laboratory operations through adherence to standards of safety and specimen collection.

Maintain and calibrate laboratory equipment, ensuring it is clean and operational, and compile and maintain records of preventive maintenance and instrument performance checks according to regulations.

Supervise or instruct laboratory staff or assistants, develop and implement training programs for trainees or medical students, and ensure laboratory quality control and assurance activities are conducted.
Archive diagnostic materials, such as histologic slides and blocks, and document case materials according to regulatory requirements.
Provide technical information about test results to physicians and assist pathologists in the preparation of reports by providing relevant clinical data or microscopic findings.
Participate in continuing education programs to enhance laboratory knowledge and practices, and train or supervise medical students, residents, or other health professionals.
Prepare standard volumetric solutions or reagents to be used in testing and ensure the accuracy of laboratory analyses by establishing quality assurance programs.
Conduct research activities related to chemical analysis and report findings, possibly including analyzing laboratory findings to validate results and conformity.
Extract, measure, dilute, label, and prepare DNA for analysis, and analyze samples for chemical content or reaction when necessary.
Examine cells stained with dyes to locate abnormalities, using microscopy when necessary to ensure quality in specimen preparation.
Perform specialized treatments or procedures in the laboratory under specified protocols, possibly to detect or analyze abnormalities or diseases within specimens.
Order, interpret or conduct diagnostic tests, specifying techniques for evaluating patients' health and treatment efficacy.

Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals; study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food; and conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, including their growth in soils and control of pests.

Diagnose illnesses, using examinations, tests, and reports. Order laboratory tests, x-rays, and other diagnostic procedures. Record type and amount of anesthesia and patient condition throughout procedure. Monitor patients' conditions and progress and reevaluate treatments as necessary. Diagnose or treat disorders of the musculoskeletal system and diseases of female organs. Examine patients using equipment to determine the nature and extent of disorder or injury. Prescribe or administer therapy, medication, and other specialized medical care to treat or prevent illness, disease, or injury. Prepare and submit case reports or summaries to government or mental health agencies. Provide consultation to physicians or health care professionals regarding treatment plans. Analyze records, reports, test results, or examination information to diagnose medical condition of patients. Collect, record, and maintain patient information, such as medical histories and examination results. Evaluate and manage chronic pain conditions. Discuss patients' treatment plans with other medical professionals. Develop new food items for production, based on consumer feedback. Research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; and study methods to improve aspects of foods, such as chemical composition and nutritional value.

Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application, and present findings to the scientific audience and the public. Conduct literature reviews and write articles for publication in professional journals. Write grant proposals to obtain funding for research. Stay up to date on new regulations and current events regarding food science. Prepare technical and research reports, such as environmental impact reports, and communicate results to stakeholders. Develop and maintain liaisons with individuals and agencies to encourage cooperative management strategies for food science solutions.

Check raw ingredients for maturity or stability for processing and finished products for safety, quality, and nutritional value. Test new products for flavor, texture, color, nutritional content, and adherence to standards. Prepare and analyze samples to study effects of drugs, gases, pesticides, or microorganisms on cell structure and tissue. Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne diseases. Conduct research on methodologies and procedures for medical applications, analyzing data and presenting findings.

Consult with and advise physicians, educators, researchers, and government officials regarding medical applications of sciences such as physics, biology, and chemistry. Supervise laboratory teams, staff, and other scientists. Plan, administer, and evaluate health safety standards and programs to improve public health.

Conduct research to develop new drugs or medications intended for commercial distribution. Develop food standards and production specifications, safety regulations, and waste management guidelines. Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause, risk factors, and mode of transmission. Analyze characteristics of animals to identify and classify them. Study the chemistry of living processes and investigate effects of management practices on animal product quality.

Study nutritional requirements of animals and nutritive values of animal feed materials. Advise producers on improved products and techniques to enhance production. Research transformations of substances in cells and monitor tests on water, food, and the environment for harmful microorganisms or sources of contamination.

Develop new methods to study biological processes. Conduct genetic research and experiments involving molecular techniques to isolate, analyze, or synthesize compounds that affect bodily functions. Maintain accurate laboratory records and communicate research results through scientific reports and presentations.

Prepare reports or recommendations based on research outcomes. Collaborate with biologists and other professionals for genetic and biochemical analyses. Study effects of environmental conditions on plants and animals, and participate in developing pest management and control measures.

Manage laboratory responsibilities, evaluate new technologies, and ensure compliance with research protocols. Supervise and train personnel in laboratory safety techniques. Prepare samples for analysis and execute experimental designs while monitoring laboratory techniques.

Study characteristics of organisms, including their diseases, genetics, and interrelationships. Teach principles of medicine and laboratory procedures to students and technicians. Collaborate on health safety standards and public health improvement programs.

Analyze large molecular datasets for clinical or basic research and manipulate genomic databases. Evaluate genetic data and perform statistical analyses to derive conclusions from research findings. Seek substitutes for harmful additives in food production.

Develop methods for securing representative samples, and design experiments with specialized equipment. Participate in research on agricultural practices that increase crop yield and quality, and improve livestock management practices.

Investigate environmental effects of land and water use. Study aquatic life and conditions impacting their ecosystems. Conduct administrative duties related to biological research programs including fundraising and public relations activities.
Evaluate public health issues related to food safety. Conduct research concerning public health, disease prevention, and methods for enhancing food safety and quality.

Prepare and analyze samples of various organic materials to detect toxicity or organisms harmful to plants, animals, and humans. Examine physiological and morphological characteristics of microorganisms to classify them.

Study mutations leading to diseases, and investigate the cancerous transformations in organisms. Explore the genetic composition of populations to determine heritability and develop breeding programs to enhance desirable traits.

Conduct laboratory procedures such as DNA sequencing and perform biological assays to monitor cell characteristics and behavior. Develop guidelines for laboratory protocols and manage research activities effectively.

Study environmental factors influencing plant and animal growth, including pest management methods. Maintain cultures of microorganisms under controlled conditions for experimental purposes, ensuring optimal growth conditions.
Study the relationship between organisms and their environments, including ecological impacts and disease control measures. Develop protocols to improve genetic techniques and diagnostics.
Engage in experimental studies with live animals. Teach principles of biochemistry to students or fellow researchers. Collaborate on interdisciplinary research efforts.

Develop statistical models for genetic data analysis, and design bioinformatics algorithms for research applications. Customize bioinformatics tools for specific project needs and create or modify bioinformatics databases.
Analyze biological data regarding organisms' relationships with their environments in the context of agricultural research for crop improvement. Investigate genetic influences on disease.
Develop new products and procedures for sterilization, food and pharmaceutical supply preservation, or microbial contamination detection.

Communicate research findings on various types of diseases to health practitioners, policy makers, and the public; educate healthcare workers, patients, and the public about infectious and communicable diseases, including disease transmission and prevention; and monitor and report incidents of infectious diseases to local and state health agencies.

Keep abreast of new biochemistries, instrumentation, or software by reading scientific literature and attending professional conferences; develop methods to process, store, or use foods, drugs, or chemical compounds; and confer with departments, such as marketing, business development, or operations, to coordinate product development or improvement.

Research the chemical effects of substances, such as drugs, serums, hormones, or food, on tissues or vital processes and study physical principles of living cells or organisms and their electrical or mechanical energy, applying methods and knowledge of mathematics, physics, chemistry, or biology.

Research how characteristics of plants or animals are carried through successive generations and investigate the nature, composition, or expression of genes or research how genetic engineering can impact these processes.

Provide statistical and computational tools for biologically based activities, such as genetic analysis, measurement of gene expression, or gene function determination, and test new and updated bioinformatics tools and software.

Perform chemical analyses of the microorganism content of soils to determine microbial reactions or chemical mineralogical relationships to plant growth; conduct experiments to investigate the underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to the environment; and identify or classify species of insects or allied forms, such as mites or spiders.

Conduct research to determine best methods of planting, spraying, cultivating, harvesting, storing, processing, or transporting horticultural products; develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or insect pests; and provide advice regarding the development of regulatory standards for land reclamation or soil conservation.

Provide information or recommendations to farmers or other landowners regarding ways in which they can best use land, promote plant growth, or avoid or correct problems such as erosion; survey undisturbed or disturbed lands for classification, inventory, mapping, environmental impact assessments, environmental protection planning, conservation planning, or reclamation planning.

Develop environmentally safe methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or pests and study ways to improve agricultural sustainability, such as the use of new methods of composting.

Investigate responses of soils to specific management practices to determine the use capabilities of soils and the effects of alternative practices on soil productivity, identify degraded or contaminated soils and develop plans to improve their chemical, biological, or physical characteristics, and develop methods of conserving or managing soil that can be applied by farmers or forestry companies.

Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops, focusing on characteristics such as yield, quality, disease resistance, nutritional value, or adaptation to specific soils or climates; investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects; and study soil characteristics to classify soils on the basis of factors such as geographic location, landscape position, or soil properties.

Develop improved measurement techniques, soil conservation methods, soil sampling devices, or related technology; conduct experiments investigating how soil forms, changes, or interacts with land-based ecosystems or living organisms.

Conduct research into the use of plant species as green fuels or in the production of green fuels; conduct experiments regarding causes of bee diseases or factors affecting yields of nectar or pollen.

emerging_tasks.task: Conduct blood typing and antibody screening.
Cut sections of body tissues for microscopic examination, using microtomes or cryostats.
Perform quality control analyses to ensure accuracy of test results.
Select and maintain controls for stains.
Consult and direct other medical personnel on patient care.
Perform mental health evaluations to provide information to courts of law on patients' mental states.
Place invasive intravascular monitors into patients.
Process patient transfers to and from other hospitals.
Perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or to obtain information about sources of pollution, contamination, or infection and interpret the results.
Teach epidemiology to students in public health programs.
Test processing equipment to ensure products are produced according to specifications.
Use advanced technologies, such as GIS, remote sensing, and drone technology, for wildlife tracking, habitat mapping, and population studies.
